title: Arogyapacha
focus: Medicinal properties, Kani tribes, and the Jeevani drug.
rationale: Distinct indigenous medicinal plant with specific tribal association (Kani) and a benefit-sharing model (Jeevani drug).
sectionExcerpt: A rare, indigenous medicinal plant of India. Medicinal Properties: Anti-oxidant, antistress, anti-microbial, anti-inflammatory, anti-tumor, anti-ulcer, anti-diabetic etc. Famous for traditional use by Kani tribes, in Agastya hills, Western Ghats. 'Jeevani' Drug has been developed using 'Arogya Pacha'. Kani Tribes gets a share of 50% on commercial returns.
evidenceBullets: Used traditionally by Kani tribes in Agastya hills, Western Ghats.
Kani tribes receive a 50% share of commercial returns.

title: Conocarpus Trees
focus: Invasive nature, environmental impact, and government bans.
rationale: An invasive ornamental plant with significant negative ecological impacts (groundwater consumption, allergenic pollen) leading to state bans.
sectionExcerpt: Recently government-appointed panel recommended Supreme Court to ban Conocarpus. (Banned earlier by Gujarat and Tamil Nadu). Has 2 species: One native to coastal areas of tropical America, and parts of West Africa. Other found in arid coastal zones of Somalia and Yemen... Ornamental plant provides green canopy... Invasive (also known as green desert); excessive groundwater consumption; as allergenic pollen, etc.
evidenceBullets: Banned by Gujarat and Tamil Nadu, with a panel recommending a Supreme Court ban.
Native to coastal areas of tropical America, Africa, and the Arabian Peninsula.
Causes excessive groundwater consumption and produces allergenic pollen.

title: Sea Buckthorn
focus: Ecological significance, nutritional value, and space experiments.
rationale: A high-altitude plant with unique ecological (pioneer species) and nutritional benefits, recently featured in ISS experiments.
sectionExcerpt: Its seeds grown in Ladakh are part of experiments on board the International Space Station flown by NASA’s Crew-11 mission. Deciduous shrub/tree; Well-adapted to live in cold areas of high altitude. Significance: Pioneer species for soil improvement, wind & sand control, and soil & water conservation; High nutritional value (vitamins; carotenoids; polyphenols, etc.); Medicinal (antioxidant; anticancer, etc.)
evidenceBullets: Seeds grown in Ladakh used in experiments on the International Space Station (NASA Crew-11).
Deciduous shrub/tree adapted to cold, high-altitude areas.
Acts as a pioneer species for soil improvement, wind/sand control, and soil/water conservation.
High nutritional and medicinal value (antioxidant, anticancer).

title: Senna Spectabilis
focus: Invasive characteristics and eradication efforts in Kerala.
rationale: A highly invasive species in the Western Ghats (Wayanad) that alters soil chemistry and chokes native flora.
sectionExcerpt: Kerala carried out India’s first science-based, community-driven eradication of Senna Spectabilis in Wayanad. Invasive species, Native to American tropicals. Forms dense, sterile thickets, choking out native plants, altering soil chemistry and depriving herbivores of food, grows 7-18m tall. Resembles Kerala’s state flower kanikkonna.
evidenceBullets: India's first science-based, community-driven eradication carried out in Wayanad, Kerala.
Invasive species native to American tropicals.
Forms dense thickets that choke native plants and alter soil chemistry.
Resembles kanikkonna, the state flower of Kerala.
